project_description.value: 163-fixture, 5-story mixed-use multifamily building — plumbing scope (underground, rough-in, trim, gas, water main)

contract_document.value: Subcontract #201301ONELLC — Lump Sum $1,394,655.00 (AGC of Washington 2006 Ed.)
contract_document.source: Change Orders/8th Ave OWP change order 1.pdf header

po_commitments.description: Fixture upgrades: elongated bowls, Grohe 34270 faucets, Aquatic tub/shower, 8" kitchen sinks Type B
po_commitments.source: Change Orders/8th Ave OWP change order 1.pdf

insights.title: Strong 38.3% net margin
insights.finding: Project closed at $533,274.30 net profit on $1,391,455.00 revenue — 38.3% margin, well above OWP's 25–30% target.

insights.title: Labor came in well under budget
insights.finding: Total labor (codes 011, 100–145) actual $291,321.88 vs $1,394,655 total contract — labor was 20.9% of revenue. Supervision (100) used 74% of budget, Roughin Labor (120) used 127%.

insights.title: Material was largest cost bucket
insights.finding: Material spend $390,750.86 = 28.1% of revenue, across 17 vendors. Rosen Supply and Ferguson were the top two suppliers.

insights.title: 15 pay apps over 16 months
insights.finding: Billed $1,393,316.00 gross across 15 pay apps from Mar 2013 to Jul 2014. Retainage of $69,572.75 held back (5% per contract).

insights.title: Contract stability — 2 COs net -$3,200 (-0.23% of original)
insights.finding: Only 2 change orders over 16 months: CO#1 +$11,100 for fixture upgrades (6/17/13) and CO#2 -$14,300 for firestopping descope (1/16/14). Scope was exceptionally stable for a $1.4M multifamily plumbing job.

insights.title: Won below bid — $1,436,800 bid → $1,394,655 awarded (2.9% concession)
insights.finding: Adjusted bid total (Plumbing Bid Sum 10/01/12) was $1,436,800 but OWP accepted $1,394,655 — a $42,145 concession (~2.9%). Despite this, project still delivered 38.3% net margin, signaling conservative bid estimating.
insights.source: Bid Sheets/220000 - Plumbing Bid Sum with OWP markups.pdf p.5

direct_cost.source: Σ labor + material + OH + burden + tax

change_log.subject: Original subcontract signed — $1,394,655 (AGC of WA 2006 Ed., Subcontract #201301ONELLC)
CO #1 — Fixture upgrades (elongated bowls, Grohe 34270 faucets, Aquatic tub/shower, 8" kitchen sinks in Type B units)
change_log.source_document: Change Orders/8th Ave OWP change order 1.pdf

root_cause_analysis.observation: Labor came in significantly under budget
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Strong crew continuity (Gerard supervision consistent throughout) and stable scope
root_cause_analysis.mitigation: Codify crew tier mix (1 foreman / N journeymen) for similar-size multifamily

root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Well-defined AGC of Washington subcontract, experienced GC
root_cause_analysis.mitigation: Target similar GC/contract template on future bids

predictive_signals.historical_outcome: Scope stability correlated with margin on 2012

reconciliation.check_description: Contract Final (JDR) = Contract Total (last pay app)
Sum of CO cost_impact = Contract Final - Contract Original
